answer:
The Harvest V2 API uses either Personal Access Tokens or OAuth2 for authentication [1]. Personal Access Tokens are generated in the Developers section of Harvest ID and are recommended for personal scripts [1]. For public integrations, you must implement an OAuth2 flow (Authorization Code for server-side or Implicit Grant for client-side) [1].

Every request requires the `Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN` and `Harvest-Account-Id: $ACCOUNT_ID` headers, along with a custom `User-Agent` string [1][2][3].

Example header authentication:
```bash
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN" \
     -H "Harvest-Account-Id: $ACCOUNT_ID" \
     -H "User-Agent: MyApp (you@example.com)" \
     https://api.harvestapp.com/v2/users/me
```
